title: Residual causal sequence expert, blended rather than replacing GBDT
summary: AutoF1 reports that an RNN trained on historic race time series can support pit-stop strategy and explicitly suggests transformer-style positional modeling for pit-stop patterns.
rationale: AutoF1 reports that an RNN trained on historic race time series can support pit-stop strategy and explicitly suggests transformer-style positional modeling for pit-stop patterns. At the same time, FT-Transformer results show there is still no universal winner between deep tabular models and GBDT, which argues for a residual sequence expert rather than a full model swap.
implementation_hint: Build per-driver-per-race causal sequences using only past laps. Feed recent standardized lap features plus the strong tree-model score into a lightweight GRU, TCN, or small transformer encoder, and train it to predict residual error on top of the tabular baseline. Blend its OOF predictions with the baseline via rank averaging.
expected_effect: Captures temporal motifs that fixed handcrafted windows miss, while keeping variance lower than a standalone sequence model.
risk: Implementation complexity is highest here; order, padding, and fold leakage can produce fake gains very easily.
